ab: an paelipas dra karealriyan an daman i, marepanauwa ta maw?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎？
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: aiwa, marepanau ta isuwa?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面？
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: marepanauwa ta i takesian i? aru lalu-ing ta.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎？我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: inaba an muka ta i palakuwan. piakiruwan ku dra bulretrenganan muka.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: an miaputr na sakura kaniyam drekalr i, salaw bulay! an mubaba lra i, alamu lra kaniyam!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來我家吧！
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: ngangara ku palru dra kaseberan lra. paetreng lra dradru!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚(久)了吧！
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: an mangayaw i, alamu lra i!
ch: W: 那你大獵祭(年底祭儀)的時候來吧！
en: Then come during our The hunting festival!

ab: aiwa i! kiyalrimana ku!
ch: M: 好啊！我很期待！
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: kiyumalra ku driya, daw muasalr yu muruma i drekalr?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活？
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: uwaruma ku patakesi.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: daw adri yu kadru i sinki patakesi?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書？
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: masupeng ku i kadrekalran. aw maranger ku pasuba dra drekalr.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: saigu yu temenun. i manay na paladam kanu?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的？
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: i mulri na babayan na paladam. i mulri na babayan i, amau na marasaiguigu temenun kaniyam drekalr.
ch: W:是我的奶奶教的，奶奶是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: daw maranger yu meladaladam temenun?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布？
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: naniyam kakuwayanan na temenun. maranger ku tremungutrungulr dra kakuwayanan.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: salaw dalreu an tremekel ta kandri na dinuliyan i!
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝！
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: kuyku na temueraw!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔！
en: I made it myself!

ab: saigu yu i! paetreng an temueraw ta dar?
ch: M:你真厲害！這要釀很久嗎？
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: adri, kararuwa dra saliyusan. maruwa ta lra emabak dra dalilr tremekel. ta tauraw driya demeru na drinereman na driketran. an maderu lra ta palritekay na bineraberas. amerasay dra saima dra enay aw paredraulaw dra pikkak. abakaw kana gulrigulriyan aw retraanay kana mubekebek.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就可以裝瓶喝了呢！把浸泡過的糯米蒸熟後放冷，撒下少許的冷開水將糯米飯攪和開來，再加入酒麴攪拌均勻後裝入甕裡，然後靜置在隱密不通風的地方。
en: No, it only takes about a week. Steam the soaked glutinous rice and let it cool. Sprinkle a little cold water over the rice to mix it up, then add the koji (rice malt) to the rice, stir it well, and pack it into an urn.
